2014.notes: Traditional format. Last World Cup before VAR. Brazil hosted in South America.

2018.notes: First World Cup with VAR. Host Russia ranked 70th, reached quarter-finals.

2022.extra_time_subs: 6th substitution allowed in extra time, plus concussion substitute
2022.notes: First World Cup in Middle East. 5-subs rule dramatically changed tactics. Semi-automated offside introduced. Host Qatar ranked 50th, finished 0 points.

2026.notes: First 48-team World Cup. 12 groups of 4, top 2 + 8 best 3rd-placed advance to 32-team knockout. Significantly more matches (104) = more upset opportunities. Three host nations across CONCACAF.

rule_change_timeline.impact: More penalties awarded (29 in 2018 vs 13 in 2014). More offside reviews. Slightly benefits defensive underdogs who can hold out
DEPTH matters more. Teams with strong benches can rotate heavily. Japan's '田忌赛马' strategy (hold first half, attack with fresh subs second half). Upsets increased 57% from 2018
FIFA instructed referees to precisely count all stoppages. 525 extra minutes in group stage alone (~6 extra matches). Less time-wasting by favored teams
Faster offside reviews. 'Hairline offside' calls increased. Slightly disrupted defensive lines
More squad depth enabled aggressive substitutions

rule_change_timeline.change: Penalty kick rule: goalkeeper one foot on line
rule_change_timeline.impact: Slightly favors goalkeepers in penalty shootouts
More minnows in tournament. Group stage has lower average quality. More matches = more upset opportunities. 3rd-place advancement adds strategic complexity
